summary.intent: Fix Dioxus ARIA ordering issues identified in a Codex review on PR #731 of fogodev/ars-ui, specifically description order independence for `field::Description` and root ARIA for `fieldset::Description`.
summary.outcome: Both Codex P2 findings were fixed by pre-seeding description state when direct `Description` child components are detected in the Dioxus adapter tree. Tests were added for each fix, adapter parity remained green, and the changes were committed and pushed.

repo: The `chatgpt-codex-connector` bot sometimes posts review findings as plain PR comments rather than review threads, which can be missed when only polling for review threads.
The repo uses `cargo xtask ci feature-matrix-dioxus` to run Dioxus feature combination tests locally, mirroring the CI matrix.
Adapter parity is tracked via `cargo xtask lint adapter-parity` and must remain green after adding new adapter-level tests.
The core component machines already have `SetHasDescription` support; the SSR ordering problem was Dioxus-adapter-specific and required a child scan fix in the adapter layer.
CI uses a multi-stage matrix; Dioxus feature flag failures can be transient network issues (crates.io HTTP2 framing errors) unrelated to code correctness.

code.finding: Pre-seeds `has_description` state by scanning direct child tree for the concrete adapter `Description` part at initial render, fixing ARIA attribute generation when Input is rendered before Description in SSR.
Pre-seeds description state similarly to fix root `aria-describedby` ARIA association for `fieldset::Description`.
Added test `field_input_references_description_when_rendered_before_description` to cover Input-before-Description ordering, which was previously untested.
Added test `fieldset_renders_group_anatomy` covering root ARIA describedby association for fieldset description.
Core field machine has no `Props::has_description`; description state is only set via `SetHasDescription` events, confirming the fix belongs in the adapter layer.

workflow: When CI job logs are unavailable (workflow still running), reproducing the exact xtask stage locally is an effective substitute for diagnosing failures.
Transient CI failures (network fetch errors from crates.io) should be confirmed as non-reproducible locally before rerunning, not fixed with code changes.
Waiting for the full workflow to finish before rerunning only the failed jobs avoids cascading reruns of dependent jobs.
Writing failing tests before implementing the fix (TDD) was used here to confirm the issue matched the Codex finding before applying the patch.

friction: GitHub job logs are not accessible until the overall workflow completes, forcing a local reproduction loop to diagnose failures mid-run.
The `chatgpt-codex-connector` posted review findings as plain comments instead of review threads, causing them to be missed during earlier monitoring passes.
Network sandbox restrictions blocked direct `gh run view --log` calls, requiring an API fallback for job metadata.
Long CI polling cycles (multiple 180–240s sleeps) extended the feedback loop significantly for a transient infrastructure failure.
open_items: Rerun the failed Dioxus feature job (and its dependent feature flag check) on CI after the current workflow completes — the failure was a transient crates.io network error, not a code issue.
Consider adding monitoring or notification for when Codex posts findings as plain comments vs. review threads, to avoid missing P2+ findings in future review cycles.
